description_long: This is a vintage pocket watch with a round metallic case and a prominent winding crown at the top. The front face features a white dial with black Roman numerals, two main hands, and a smaller subdial for seconds at the bottom. The glass cover is slightly convex, and the case has a tarnished, aged metallic finish, suggesting it is made of brass or a similar alloy. The back of the watch is plain and unadorned, showing signs of wear and patina. The side views reveal a hinge and a small button, likely for opening the case to access the dial or mechanism. This object exudes an antique charm and would be a collectible or decorative item in an urban setting.
description: A vintage round pocket watch with a white Roman numeral dial, metallic case, and a prominent winding crown. The watch shows signs of age and features a subdial for seconds.
description_view_0: Front view showing the white dial with black Roman numerals, two main hands, a subdial, and the winding crown at the top.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness of the metallic case, the winding crown, and a small button on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, slightly tarnished metallic back of the watch with no visible markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the case hinge and the profile of the glass cover and winding crown.
attribute_face_design: Circular white face with black Roman numerals, a subdial at the bottom, and minimal decorative elements.
